overview: Boros Energy is a Modern aggro deck that leverages the Energy mechanic to cast spells for free and establish a fast clock. It merges an Energy package centered on Amped Raptor and Guide of Souls with a blink/value strategy using Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to maximize ETB effects and card advantage.
win_conditions: Establish a fast clock with cheap creatures and Guide of Souls
Deal direct damage with Unstable Amulet and Phlage, Titan of Fire’s Fury
Generate value and board presence by blinking permanents with Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd
Cast spells for free using Amped Raptor to accelerate damage

early_game: Play cheap creatures and lands to establish a fast clock
Generate energy with Static Prison or Galvanic Discharge
Use Amped Raptor to cast early threats or removal for free
mid_game: Blink permanents with Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to trigger ETB effects and grow the board
Use Guide of Souls to make creatures threatening and bypass blockers
Deal incremental damage with Unstable Amulet while maintaining card advantage
late_game: Maintain pressure with recurring value from Phelia and Unstable Amulet
Leverage Ragavan, Nimble Pilferer and Phlage, Titan of Fire’s Fury for graveyard value and damage
Utilize sideboard cards like White Orchid Phantom or Disruptor Flute to close out specific matchups

key_cards.name: Amped Raptor
key_cards.notes: Can cast almost every spell for free; tends to come out when needing conditional responses that might whiff.

key_cards.name: Guide of Souls
key_cards.notes: Turns any creature into a threat; easily triggered by Energy to establish a fast clock.

key_cards.name: Unstable Amulet
key_cards.notes: Recurring source of advantage/damage; main target for Phelia's blink effect.

key_cards.name: Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd
key_cards.notes: Adds value with ETB effects, synergizes with Ajani and Unstable Amulet, can remove unwanted blockers.

key_cards.name: Ajani, Nacatl Pariah
key_cards.notes: Legends rule interaction creates tokens to protect it, making it highly efficient in games.

key_cards.name: Phlage, Titan of Fire’s Fury
key_cards.notes: Feeds on graveyard, sets a two-turn clock, deals up to 11 damage with Guide of Souls.

key_cards.name: Static Prison
key_cards.notes: Low mana cost, gives two energy before sacrificing itself, even without a target.

key_cards.name: Galvanic Discharge
key_cards.notes: Deals with creatures/planeswalkers; stores unused energy on smaller creatures for Unstable Amulet.

key_cards.name: Lightning Bolt
key_cards.notes: Ensures Amped Raptor doesn't whiff, speeds up clock against various threats.

key_cards.name: Fetch Lands / Aether Hub / Elegant Parlor / Inspiring Vantage
key_cards.notes: Maintains consistency, finds basics for Blood Moon, fuels Phlage, generates mana.

sequencing_tips: Blink Amped Raptor with Phelia to generate energy for triggering Guide of Souls on the following turn.
Cast Static Prison early to generate two energy before it sacrifices itself, even without a target.
Use Galvanic Discharge on smaller creatures to store excess energy for Unstable Amulet.
Save Amped Raptor for games requiring conditional responses to avoid whiffing.

matchups.advice: Side in graveyard hate and disruption to counter their win condition and apply early pressure.
Use Disruptor Flute and Wrath of the Skies to disrupt their combo and clear token boards.
Side in White Orchid Phantom to control lands and maintain aggression against their ramp.
Side in Tormod's Crypt and Unlicensed Hearse to disrupt graveyard interactions and prevent their win condition.
Use Galvanic Discharge to deal with Yawgmoth, Thran Physician early and apply pressure before they stabilize.

overview_evidence.evidence_span: Boros Energy is one of the new archetypes enabled by MH3's impact on Modern... a new variant of aggressive decks has emerged and highlighted one of the main mechanics that MH3 brought: Energy.

win_conditions_evidence.evidence_span: Guide of Souls turns any creature we have into a threat, and in an archetype focused on the Energy mechanic, it is not difficult for us to trigger its ability every turn.
Unstable Amulet is a recurring source of card advantage and damage in Boros Energy... Phlage, Titan of Fire’s Fury... can deal up to 11 damage in a single attack.
use Phelia, Exuberant Shepherd to add value along with powerful ETB effects.
Amped Raptor is one of the strongest cards in MH3 so far and can cast almost every spell on the list for free.

sequencing_tips_evidence.evidence_span: blinking an Amped Raptor is a good proposal, as it guarantees the necessary energy to trigger Guide of Souls the next turn.
Static Prison... even if there is no target in play for the enchantment, it will still give us two energy before being sacrificed the next turn.
Galvanic Discharge... in smaller creatures, we can store unused Energy to power Unstable Amulet.
Amped Raptor... tends to come out in games where we need more conditional responses that can “whiff” when played by it.

common_threats_evidence.evidence_span: Galvanic Discharge can deal with almost any creature or Planeswalker on this list, and it is common to use it to destroy Yawgmoth, Thran Physician , Nadu, Winged Wisdom or even Primeval Titan if necessary.
Wrath of the Skies... we can use it mainly to deal with Urza's Saga and Simulacrum Synthesizer 's tokens.
The pack of six Fetch Lands is enough to maintain archetype consistency and find basic lands for matches against opponents who use Blood Moon in Game 1.
